PETITIONER LING LA'S PETITION FOR RELIEF UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE 946.6
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Continued To Jan-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER LING LA'S PETITION FOR RELIEF UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE 946.6.

Appearances: Ling La (650.733.4605) petitioner and moving party appearing in propria persona; Morgan E. Lewis of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4224) for respondent and opposing party.

Argued and the court rules as follows: continued to January 16, 2026. Parties may file further briefs on the issues discussed. Respondent's further brief to be filed by January 7, 2026. Petitioner's further brief to be filed by January 13, 2026. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Adopted Tentative Ruling Pursuant To Ccp Section 473(B) And Section 473(D); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER LING LA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ADOPTED TENTATIVE RULING PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 473(B) AND SECTION 473(D).

Appearances: Ling La (650.733.4605) petitioner and moving party appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Due to the court's unavailability on December 22, 2025, this case is called on December 23, 2025. This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Ling La's motion to set aside adopted tentative ruling is granted. The order filed November 12, 2025 denying petitioner's petition for relief per Government Code 946.6 is vacated and a new hearing on that petition will be held in Department 301 on December 29, 2025. The court's minutes of November 12, 2025 state that the tentative ruling was adopted denying petitioner's petition for relief because petitioner had not timely contested the tentative ruling. Petitioner has shown good cause to vacate the order adopting the tentative ruling based on her reasonable belief that the tentative ruling would be issued by Department 302, not Department 301. The reasonableness of petitioner's belief is established by the October 21, 2025 order providing that the hearing on petitioner's petition for relief would be heard in Department 302 on November 12, 2025. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Relief Under Government Code 946.6
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances Via Zoom Conference
Ling La, 650-733-4605 Email: lingla@hotmail.com Plaintiff in Pro-Per
MORGAN E. LEWIS, ESQ., Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-4224 morgan.lewis@sfcityatty.org
Attorneys for Respondent CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

Matter was not properly contested by Pro Per Plaintiff. Notice of contest was received on November 10, 2025 at 11:19 p.m. The contesting party was under the belief that this mater was transferred to Dept. 302 per Plaintiff's 170.6 challenge to Judge Christine Van Aken. Plaintiff's motion was correctly noticed in Department 301. It was expalined to parties that the motion was decided Visiting Judge Dennis Hayashi and ramined in Dept. 301. Prior to the hearing, Ms. Lewis was informed that the matter would not be called due to being untimely contested and the Tentative Ruling would be adopted. Ms. Lewis did not remain on ZOOM. Ling La appeared on Zoom shortly thereafter. It was explained to Plaintiff that her contest was late and argument would not be held. Ms. Ling insisted to speak with the court. Court informs Plaintiff that the matter will not be heard and that the tentative ruling will be adopted.

Discussed, Not argued. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER LING LA'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Relief Under Government Code 946.6 is denied.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Proposed order is submitted.

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/DH)
